home.headlineHtml:
I build resilient software
across systems, product, and AI.
home.headlineGlitch: I build resilient software across systems, product, and AI.
home.ledeHtml: Carnegie Mellon BS+MS Electrical & Computer Engineering student with a Robotics minor, focused on full-stack product engineering, infrastructure, distributed systems, robotics, security, and applied ML.
home.stack: Python · C/C++ · Java · TypeScript · React · AWS
ticker: // recent: Atlassian admin ops UI reduced ~34 hrs/week
// prior: Amazon SDE intern built S3/DynamoDB/QuickSight metrics
// build: CUDA vision · AI authentication · networking · robotics

items.title: LONG-TAIL US LANDMARK RECOGNITION
items.description: Built a 50-class, 18,883-image landmark dataset with CLIP filtering, compared ResNet-50 and ViT-B/16 across five losses, and reached 75.72% Top-1 accuracy with energy-score OOD detection.

items.title: GPU-ACCELERATED CANNY EDGE DETECTION
items.description: Created a CUDA Canny pipeline with fused Gaussian, Sobel, and non-max suppression kernels, fp16 shared-memory optimizations, and tile-based hysteresis that reduced latency from 7.3 ms CPU to 1.8 ms GPU.

items.title: MOSAICLEDGER
items.description: Built a fintech budgeting app in a team of 3 with Plaid, Capital One Nessie, D3 treemaps, subscription detection, hosted MCP tools, XRPL round-up savings, and Vitest/Playwright CI.

items.title: CATOMBOTS SEARCH & RESCUE SWARM
items.description: Architected software for a low-cost UGV swarm using ROS pub/sub over Wi-Fi mesh, LiDAR obstacle avoidance, UWB-IR ranging, IMU fusion, frontier exploration, and a live 10 FPS operator map.

items.title: PRODUCTION MOVIE RECOMMENDATION ENGINE
items.description: Developed a personalized recommendation API for a 1M-user, 20K-title simulated streaming platform with Kafka activity logs, top-20 rankings under 600 ms, CI checks, drift validation, and offline/online metrics.

items.title: HTTP PSEUDO-STREAMING SERVER
items.description: Implemented an HTTP/1.1 server from scratch with low-level Python sockets, full method/status support, byte-range video streaming for 5 MB+ media, keep-alive concurrency, and 200+ simultaneous clients.

items.title: DEEP-LIVE-CAM AI CONTENT AUTH
items.description: Added forensic traceability to deepfake media using invisible LSB watermarking, RSA-2048/4096 signatures, encrypted key handling, verification tools, and Dockerized cross-platform ML deployment.

items.title: MONOPIX-SCOUT TRACKING PIXEL EXTENSION
items.description: Built a Chrome MV3 extension that detects and optionally blocks 1x1 pixels, sendBeacon, fetch/XHR hooks, and scripted calls with live alerts, event logs, severity badges, and Observe/Soft-Block/Strict modes.

items.title: CHRONOSCOPE XR REALITY RESKINNING
items.description: Designed a Meta Quest mixed-reality system that reskins a physical environment through draggable portals using Unity, Shader Graph, Blender, Polycam capture, generated textures, and Meshy 2D-to-3D assets.

items.title: SELF-LOCALIZING XR CONTROLLER
items.description: Fused 6-DoF AprilTag pose with 3-DoF IMU orientation, calibrated a Pi Camera with OpenCV, streamed quaternions at ~40 Hz, and re-localized tags for a live ARENA scene.

items.title: UDP-BASED RELIABLE TRANSPORT
items.description: Built a UDP file-transfer protocol with a packet format, TCP-like state machine, 5+ concurrent sessions on one port, and recovery under simulated 5% packet loss.

items.title: CONTENT DISTRIBUTION OVERLAY
items.description: Built a UDP overlay where nodes replicate shared data, detect failures with keepalives, support one-sided neighbor adds, and rebuild global topology with link-state advertisements.

items.title: SOFTWARE WI-FI RECEIVER
items.description: Implemented deinterleaving, 4-QAM demodulation, hard-decision Viterbi decoding, and a two-stage synchronizer for detection and fine offset recovery.

items.title: YACA CHAT APP
items.description: Created a full-stack chat app with authentication, friend lists, REST APIs, responsive UI, websockets, MongoDB, and real-time group chat for 20+ users.

items.title: AUTONOMOUS ROBOT NAVIGATION
items.description: Built LiDAR/odometry occupancy grids with >90% arena navigation accuracy, tuned neural perception, evaluated A*/RRT* planners, and applied behavior trees for task execution.

items.title: BUFFER POOL MANAGER
items.description: Implemented a thread-safe disk buffer pool with page fetch, eviction, pin counts, LRU-K replacement, a background disk scheduler, promise I/O callbacks, and Read/WritePageGuard RAII.

items.title: MOUNTAIN-CAR REINFORCEMENT LEARNING
items.description: Implemented approximate Q-learning for Mountain-Car, tested epsilon-greedy exploration strategies, and analyzed MDP value iteration and policy convergence.

items.title: WHITEBOARD DRAWING FRANKA ROBOT ARM
items.description: Implemented forward and inverse kinematics, Cartesian and joint-space trajectory planning, collision-aware motion, smooth interpolation, and singularity avoidance for a Franka Emika arm.

items.title: EMBEDDED ROVER
items.description: Designed a custom Fusion 360 PCB, wrote I2C/LCD/UART/servo drivers, implemented STM32 PID motor control, hardware PWM, RMS scheduling, and UART telemetry to Python visualizations.

items.title: BASEBALL DATABASE ANALYSIS
items.description: Queried and analyzed 20K+ Lahman Baseball rows using SQL and dplyr joins, aggregations, filtering, and sorting to surface player, payroll, and historical performance insights.

items.title: LOGISTIC REGRESSION SENTIMENT ANALYSIS
items.description: Built a restaurant-review sentiment analyzer with gradient descent, L2 regularization, text preprocessing, performance visualizations, and hyperparameter tuning.

items.title: DECISION TREE IMPLEMENTATION
items.description: Implemented entropy, mutual information, recursive tree construction, prediction/evaluation pipelines, depth sweeps, and decision-boundary visualizations for binary classification.

items.title: SHELL LAB
items.description: Built a Linux shell that parses and executes command lines, handles options with getopt, and manages foreground/background jobs, stopped tasks, continuing, and termination.

items.title: WEB-CACHING PROXY
items.description: Implemented a concurrent C caching web proxy compatible with Mozilla Firefox, using mutual exclusion and semaphores to coordinate client requests.

items.title: CACHE LAB + MEMORY ALLOCATOR
items.description: Built an LRU cache simulator for Valgrind traces, ranked #2 of 126 on optimized matrix transpose, and implemented a custom malloc/free/calloc allocator with segregated free lists and mini blocks.

about.ledeHtml: I'm Adrian Muñoz, a CMU electrical and computer engineer who likes turning ambiguous technical problems into reliable software people can actually use.
about.bodyHtml: My recent work spans enterprise email and notification infrastructure at Harvey AI, production admin tooling at Atlassian, AWS data-processing infrastructure at Amazon, AI model evaluation at Outlier, and projects across CUDA, networking, browser privacy, cryptography, robotics, XR, and ML systems.
signals: BS+MS in Electrical & Computer Engineering at Carnegie Mellon, minor in Robotics.

Relevant coursework: Computer Systems, Embedded Systems, XR Systems, Full-Stack Development, Computer Networks, Machine Learning, Data Structures & Algorithms, Intelligent Robot Systems, Browser Security, Statistical Computing, and Matrices.
Production experience with React, Spring Boot, REST APIs, PostgreSQL, AWS Lambda, DynamoDB, S3, QuickSight, Java services, and validation-heavy admin UX.
Developer toolkit: Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, IntelliJ, Git, Jira, Docker, MongoDB, GitHub Actions, Postman, CMake, GDB, and Linux.

items.bodyHtml: Harvey AI - Software Engineer Intern in San Francisco, responsible for email and notifications infrastructure for Enterprise Admin.

Atlassian - built a full-stack admin UI for product release-track operations with React, Spring Boot REST APIs, and PostgreSQL; reduced admin workload by ~34 hours/week across 17 engineers, improved issue resolution by 50%, and reduced input-related errors by 80%.
Outlier - created 15 coding prompts weekly and validated model outputs with automated tools plus manual checks, increasing model evaluation coverage by 17% using pandas/NumPy pass-rate measurements.

Amazon - designed a Java service for S3 file metrics, built AWS Lambda dispatcher/processor handlers, connected DynamoDB data to QuickSight dashboards, and helped 8 engineers analyze ~11,000 ASIN records.
Carnegie Mellon University - BS+MS in Electrical & Computer Engineering, Minor in Robotics, GPA 3.43/4.00.

photos.caption: // personal photo archive loaded from committed browser-safe images.

contact.disclaimer: // open to software engineering roles spanning full-stack product, infrastructure, systems, robotics, security, and applied AI/ML tooling.
